channel.title: Network-Management on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Network-Management on Pi Stack

item.title: Self-Hosted DHCP Server Management — Kea vs dnsmasq vs ISC DHCP
item.description: Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) is the invisible backbone of every network. Every device that connects — laptops, phones, IoT sensors, servers, printers — relies on DHCP to get an IP address, DNS server information, and network configuration parameters. Yet most organizations treat DHCP as an afterthought, relying on their router’s built-in server or basic configuration files with no monitoring, no centralized management, and no visibility into address allocation.

item.title: Self-Hosted DNS Management Web UIs — PowerDNS-Admin vs dnscontrol vs Technitium DNS Server
item.description: Managing DNS zones, records, and configurations through command-line tools is error-prone and time-consuming — especially in multi-domain environments. Web-based DNS management interfaces solve this by providing visual record editors, bulk import/export, API integration, and role-based access control, all while keeping your DNS infrastructure self-hosted.

item.title: Self-Hosted Network Topology Mapping — NetBox, LibreNMS & Auto-Discovery Tools
item.description: When your infrastructure grows beyond a handful of servers, keeping track of how everything connects — switches to routers, firewalls to servers, VLANs to subnets — becomes essential. Self-hosted network topology mapping tools automatically discover and visualize your network layout, generating real-time maps that show device relationships, link status, and traffic flows.
